approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `amount` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

returns._0: Timestamp of the last reserve update

params.to: Address to receive the withdrawn tokens

params.limit0: The limit0 for the token in token decimals
params.limit1: The limit1 for the token in token decimals
params.token: The token to configure limits for

params.disable: Boolean to disable initializers for implementation contract

decimals().details: Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the value {ERC20} uses, unless this function is overridden; NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}.

decreaseAllowance(address,uint256).details: Atomically decreases the allowance granted to `spender` by the caller. This is an alternative to {approve} that can be used as a mitigation for problems described in {IERC20-approve}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address. - `spender` must have allowance for the caller of at least `subtractedValue`.

returns.oraclePriceDenominator: The denominator of the oracle price.
returns.oraclePriceNumerator: The numerator of the oracle price.
returns.priceDifference: The price difference between the oracle and pool reserve prices in basis points.
returns.rebalanceThreshold: The rebalance threshold in basis points.
returns.reservePriceAboveOraclePrice: Whether the pool reserve price is above the oracle price.
returns.reservePriceDenominator: The denominator of the pool reserve price.
returns.reservePriceNumerator: The numerator of the pool reserve price.

returns.config: Trading limits config for the token
returns.state: Trading limits state for the token

increaseAllowance(address,uint256).details: Atomically increases the allowance granted to `spender` by the caller. This is an alternative to {approve} that can be used as a mitigation for problems described in {IERC20-approve}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

params._invertRateFeed: Whether to invert the rate feed
params._referenceRateFeedID: Address of the reference rate feed ID

returns._0: Whether the address is a trusted liquidity strategy

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

rebalance(uint256,uint256,bytes).details: Only callable by approved liquidity strategies

returns._0: Rebalance threshold above in basis points
Rebalance threshold below in basis points
Address of the reference rate feed ID

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions anymore. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby removing any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params._invertRateFeed: Whether to invert the rate feed

params._protocolFeeRecipient: The recipient of the protocol fee

params._rebalanceThresholdAbove: New threshold above in basis points
params._rebalanceThresholdBelow: New threshold below in basis points

params._referenceRateFeedID: Address of the reference rate feed

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `amount`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. This is not required by the EIP. See the note at the beginning of {ERC20}. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `amount`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `amount`.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

BASIS_POINTS_DENOMINATOR().notice: Returns the denominator for basis point calculations (10000 = 100%)

MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY().notice: Returns the minimum liquidity that will be locked forever when creating a pool

TRADING_MODE_BIDIRECTIONAL().notice: Returns the mode value for bidirectional trading from circuit breaker

blockTimestampLast().notice: Returns the timestamp of the last reserve update

burn(address).notice: Burns LP tokens to withdraw liquidity from the pool

configureTradingLimit(address,uint256,ui.notice: Configure trading limits for a token

decimals0().notice: Returns the scaling factor for token0 based on its decimals

decimals1().notice: Returns the scaling factor for token1 based on its decimals

getAmountOut(uint256,address).notice: Calculates output amount for a given input

getRebalancingState().notice: Gets the rebalancing state of the pool

getTradingLimits(address).notice: Gets trading limits config and state for a token

invertRateFeed().notice: Returns the invert rate feed flag

liquidityStrategy(address).notice: Checks if an address is a trusted liquidity strategy

lpFee().notice: Returns the LP fee in basis points (1 basis point = .01%)

mint(address).notice: Mints LP tokens by providing liquidity to the pool

protocolFee().notice: Returns the protocol fee in basis points (1 basis point = .01%)

protocolFeeRecipient().notice: Returns the recipient of the protocol fee

rebalance(uint256,uint256,bytes).notice: Rebalances the pool to align with oracle price

rebalanceIncentive().notice: Returns the slippage allowed for rebalance operations in basis points

rebalanceThresholdAbove().notice: Returns the threshold for triggering rebalance when reserve price > oracle price in basis points

rebalanceThresholdBelow().notice: Returns the threshold for triggering rebalance when reserve price < oracle price in basis points

referenceRateFeedID().notice: Returns the reference rate feed ID to query for oracle price

reserve0().notice: Returns the reserve amount of token0

reserve1().notice: Returns the reserve amount of token1

setInvertRateFeed(bool).notice: Sets the invert rate feed flag

setReferenceRateFeedID(address).notice: Sets the reference rate feed ID

token0().notice: Returns the address of the first token in the pair

token1().notice: Returns the address of the second token in the pair

tokens().notice: Returns addresses of both tokens in the pair
